name: VCBCommentBlock
description: Adds an editor-only Comment Block to Virtual Circuit Board. Pick the comment block like any other ink — it's in the palette's Annotation row (between Filler and None) and in the Q/A quick menu — then draw comment blocks on the board (they are never simulated). Click a block to open a popup and type a comment; hover to read it as a tooltip that follows the mouse with a soft fade. Adjacent blocks merge into one bigger comment. Move a whole comment with the selection tool (it can't be dropped over another edited comment). Comments hide while you use a drawing tool so you can draw circuit under them, and during simulation they show on hover only when the 'Show comments' checkbox is on. Drawing long comments stays smooth. Fully multiplayer-synced (placements, moves and live text) and saved inside the .vcb file. Pure GDScript, loads at runtime, never replaces vcb.pck.
